Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search Slack #engineering (C003) for Dmitri's own message about the validation test expansion and Canopy config gap. Tool: conversations_search_messages with search_query "Canopy" or "validation" or "config", filter_in_channel C003. Finds Dmitri's message (moveops_dmitri_volkov, ts 1777021200, April 24) reporting Canopy Health has no custom policy config, BrightLoop passes cleanly, and Dr. Yusuf Abdi's $749 standing desk shows as Approved with no policy applied. Message has reply_count 1.
2. Retrieve the thread reply on Dmitri's #engineering message. Tool: conversations_replies with channel_id C003 and thread_ts 1777021200. Finds Lena's reply (moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, ts 1777024800, April 24) revealing that the auto-disable deployed on March 18 only targeted the Vectral and Mosaic policy configs. Any non-pilot client like Canopy would still have default auto-approve behavior for anything under the template limits. This explains how the Abdi submission got approved without review.
3. Search Airtable for Canopy Health stipend submissions. Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name "Stipend Transactions", field_name "Account", value "Canopy Health". Finds multiple records for Dr. Yusuf Abdi under Canopy Health, including recStip000000001: Standing Desk, $749, Approval Status Approved, Approved Amount $749, Review Notes empty, dated April 22; recStip000000002: Dual Monitor Setup, $1120, Partially Approved, $800 approved; recStip000000003: Internet Setup, $150, Approved; and recStip000000012: Ergonomic Keyboard, $199, Approved. The key finding is that the $749 standing desk was auto-approved with no policy config and empty review notes.
4. Search emails for any Canopy Health stipend or approval concerns from the account side. Tool: search_emails with query "Canopy" or "stipend" or "Abdi" or "approval". Finds Catalina Dubois's email (email_catalina_canopy_stipend_concern) to samira.tariq@moveops.com reporting she never received a review notification for the Abdi approval, questions whether Canopy was set up on the stipend platform, and notes Patricia Langford at Canopy asked about it.
5. Search Slack #finance (C005) for any finance flags on the Canopy payment. Tool: conversations_search_messages with search_query "Canopy" or "Abdi" or "749" or "auto-approve", filter_in_channel C005. Finds Marcus Thorne's message (moveops_marcus_thorne, ts 1777034400, April 24) asking whether auto-approve was re-enabled since a Canopy $749 payment was processed automatically. Message has reply_count 1.
6. Retrieve the thread reply on Marcus's #finance message. Tool: conversations_replies with channel_id C005 and thread_ts 1777034400. Finds Hana Kim's reply (moveops_hana_kim, ts 1777037400, April 24) revealing that Canopy Health was never formally onboarded to the stipend platform. No signed stipend platform agreement, no policy configuration request from Catalina, and no onboarding ticket in Linear. The system accepted the submission because there is no access control checking whether a client is enrolled in the stipend program.
7. Retrieve the ENG-212 ticket (issue_edge_cases_backlog) to understand the edge case testing scope before adding the new finding. Tool: linear_get_issue with id issue_edge_cases_backlog. Finds ENG-212: "Remaining edge case coverage -- policy config validation & malformed data handling", assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov, state in_progress, with 6 priority test cases and 3 nice-to-have items.
8. Look up the Canopy Health account to identify the account manager. Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name "Client Accounts", field_name "Client", value "Canopy Health", or crm_search_companies with name "Canopy". Finds Account Manager: Catalina Dubois.
9. Look up Catalina Dubois's contact details for the conditional email. Tool: contacts_search_contacts with query "Catalina Dubois". Finds catalina.dubois@moveops.com.
10. Update ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog) with the Canopy config gap as a new finding. Tool: linear_create_comment with issueId issue_edge_cases_backlog, or linear_update_issue with id issue_edge_cases_backlog to add the finding to the description. The update should document that Canopy Health has no policy config, the Abdi submission was auto-approved against the default template, and this represents a test coverage gap for non-pilot clients.
11. Post a message in #engineering (C003) so Samira sees the finding before standup. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003. The message should summarize the Canopy config gap, the Abdi auto-approval, and the implications for the platform.
12. Send an email to Catalina Dubois from Dmitri if the investigation determines she does not already know about the issue. Tool: send_email with sender dmitri.volkov@moveops.com and recipients catalina.dubois@moveops.com. Note: Catalina's email to Samira (email_catalina_canopy_stipend_concern) shows she already flagged the issue, so the agent may determine the email is not needed. If the agent does send, the email should flag the config gap and the unapproved platform usage.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1. Search Slack #engineering (C003) for Dmitri's own message about the validation test expansion and Canopy config gap. Message has reply_count 1.
2. Retrieve the thread reply on Dmitri's #engineering message. Tool: conversations_replies with channel_id C003 and thread_ts 1777021200. Finds Lena's reply (moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, ts 1777024800, April 24) revealing that the auto-disable deployed on March 18 only targeted the Vectral and Mosaic policy configs. Any non-pilot client like Canopy would still have default auto-approve behavior for anything under the template limits. This explains how the Abdi submission got approved without review.
3. Search Airtable for Canopy Health stipend submissions. Tool: airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name "Stipend Transactions", field_name "Account", value "Canopy Health". The key finding is that the $749 standing desk was auto-approved with no policy config and empty review notes.
4. Search emails for any Canopy Health stipend or approval concerns from the account side. Tool: search_emails with query "Canopy" or "stipend" or "Abdi" or "approval". 6. Retrieve the thread reply on Marcus's #finance message. Tool: conversations_replies with channel_id C005 and thread_ts 1777034400. No signed stipend platform agreement, no policy configuration request from Catalina, and no onboarding ticket in Linear. The system accepted the submission because there is no access control checking whether a client is enrolled in the stipend program.
7. Retrieve the ENG-212 ticket (issue_edge_cases_backlog) to understand the edge case testing scope before adding the new finding. Tool: linear_get_issue with id issue_edge_cases_backlog. Finds ENG-212: "Remaining edge case coverage -- policy config validation & malformed data handling", assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov, state in_progress, with 6 priority test cases and 3 nice-to-have items.

9. Look up Catalina Dubois's contact details for the conditional email. Tool: contacts_search_contacts with query "Catalina Dubois". Finds catalina.dubois@moveops.com.
10. Update ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog) with the Canopy config gap as a new finding. Tool: linear_create_comment with issueId issue_edge_cases_backlog, or linear_update_issue with id issue_edge_cases_backlog to add the finding to the description. The update should document that Canopy Health has no policy config, the Abdi submission was auto-approved against the default template, and this represents a test coverage gap for non-pilot clients.
11. Post a message in #engineering (C003) so Samira sees the finding before standup. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003. The message should summarize the Canopy config gap, the Abdi auto-approval, and the implications for the platform.
12. Send an email to Catalina Dubois from Dmitri if the investigation determines she does not already know about the issue. 5 The final response identifies that the auto-disable deployed after the pilot pause only targeted the Vectral and Mosaic policy configs, meaning non-pilot clients like Canopy would still have default auto-approve behavior (or similar).

✓Run #2The final response states: 'the auto-disable (auto_decisions_enabled: false) was only applied to Vectral and Mosaic. Lena's 3/18 deployment confirmation explicitly states: Auto-approval/denial has been turned off for Vectral and Mosaic.' This correctly identifies that non-pilot clients like Canopy still had default auto-approve behavior.
✓Run #3The final response clearly states 'The auto-disable only covers the two pilot clients. Samira's remediation added auto_decisions_enabled: false to the Vectral and Mosaic configs specifically.' This correctly identifies that non-pilot clients like Canopy were not covered.
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states: 'the `auto_decisions_enabled: false` flag was added only to Vectral and Mosaic configs.' It confirms Canopy is 'completely outside the scope of the safeguard' since it has no config and therefore no flag, matching the criterion's requirement.
✓Run #5The final response clearly states: 'Confirmed from Lena's deploy message on 3/18: she explicitly set auto_decisions_enabled: false for Vectral and Mosaic only.' The #engineering channel history (C003) confirms Lena's message about disabling auto-decisions only for those two pilot clients.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states 'she explicitly set `auto_decisions_enabled: false` for Vectral and Mosaic only. Canopy was never a pilot client, so the flag was never applied.' 6 The final response identifies that someone on the account side or in finance already flagged the Canopy stipend issue, such as Catalina Dubois raising it in email or Marcus Thorne questioning it in #finance (or similar).
outcome

✗Run #1The final response states 'No. Nobody — not Catalina Dubois, not finance, not ops — has raised this. You're the first person to notice.' ✗Run #2The final response explicitly states 'No. Nobody has mentioned Canopy in the context of missing policy configs, expense processing issues, or the auto-categorizer. You are the first person to catch this.' However, per the criterion, Catalina Dubois and Marcus Thorne had already flagged the issue. The agent missed this prior flagging.
✗Run #3The final response concludes 'No. I searched Slack (all channels), email, and Linear extensively. No one has raised the missing Canopy config.' The agent failed to identify that Catalina Dubois or Marcus Thorne had already flagged this issue. ✗Run #4The final response explicitly states 'Nobody flagged this before you' and 'zero mentions of Canopy's missing config.' The agent failed to discover that Catalina Dubois had already emailed Samira or that Marcus Thorne had already flagged the $749 payment in #finance. The agent did not check the #finance channel history or find existing cross-channel discussions about this issue.
✗Run #5The agent concluded 'Nobody has flagged this yet — I'm the first to catch this.' The criterion states that Catalina and Marcus had already flagged it. After reviewing the full C005 (#finance) channel history and email search results, no messages from Catalina or Marcus specifically flagging the Canopy stipend config issue were found in the data, indicating the agent either missed them or they were not available. The final response fails to report prior flagging.
✗Run #6The final response states 'Nobody has flagged this yet... You are the first person to notice this.' However, the criteria indicates that Catalina Dubois and Marcus Thorne had already flagged the Canopy stipend issue. The agent searched C005, various email threads, and Slack but failed to find these prior flags, reporting an incorrect conclusion.
